feedback.text: Ladies and gentlemen, I would like to point out that, in my opinion, companies have established themselves as usual in the area of video game companies. The basic idea is that the customer is not selling the video game, but only a digital licence with access to the video game, which can at any time be invalidated by the video game seller for an unlimited period and without justification. The customer is not explicitly informed of this at the time of purchase, on the contrary, this is usually hidden in the User Agreement, with the intention that, as far as possible, nobody can read it. In theory, this means that the customer thinks that he would acquire property even though he only acquires arbitrarily expropriable possession. This is a huge difference. For more details, see the citizens’ initiative entitled ‘Stop Killing Games’ ECI(2024)000007, which has already reached over 1.4 million signatures from EU citizens. Thank you for your attention and I hope that the many comments make a difference here.
